text.title: AI-Assisted Requirements Elicitation Core Rules
text.summary: Five hard rules for BAs using LLMs to draft requirements in regulated / high-stakes domains.

rule.statement: Every AI-drafted user story and acceptance criterion carries an evidence citation pointing to ≥ 1 elicitation transcript / workshop note with source_id + line range. Requirements without citations are REJECTED before human review.

rationale.source: BABOK v3 ch. 9 on requirements analysis; Anthropic Claude citations API

rule.rationale: Untraceable requirements are the BA's nightmare in audit. Citations make every requirement defensible: the BA can show which stakeholder asked for which behavior. Without citations the LLM is "interpreting" intent which is fiction, not requirements engineering.

rule.statement: Stories use BABOK v3 / agile-compliant format: "As a [persona], I want [capability], so that [outcome]." Acceptance criteria use Given/When/Then OR scenario-table format. Free-form prose requirements are rejected. The template is the contract with downstream developers and testers.

rationale.source: IIBA BABOK Guide v3; Mike Cohn user-story patterns

rule.rationale: Structured format makes downstream work (estimation, testing, traceability) possible. LLMs default to flowing prose which breaks the pipeline. Forcing the template makes the AI a drafting assistant, not a free essayist.

rule.statement: When the engagement is in a compliance-relevant domain (HIPAA, PCI, SOX, GDPR, AML, MiCA, etc.), every AI draft passes through a "compliance-cross-check" step: the requirement is matched against a documented constraint list; conflicting drafts are flagged for human review BEFORE inclusion in the spec.

rationale.source: regulatory-risk research; financial-services AI governance frameworks

rule.rationale: LLMs trained on general data may produce requirements that violate domain regulations (e.g., a logging requirement that captures PHI). The cross-check is the catch. Without it the BA discovers the conflict in a later compliance review, having already coded against the wrong requirement.

rule.statement: EVERY AI-drafted requirement is reviewed by the BA within the engagement's SLA (typical: 24h). Review is hands-on: BA verifies citation matches, checks AC completeness, edits language. No batch approve. BA signs the review log per requirement.
rule.rationale: The BA carries professional responsibility under IIBA standards. Auto-approved AI requirements are an ethics violation. The per-requirement review log creates the paper trail for audit + a quality signal for the LLM (which requirements needed major edits → improve the prompt).

rule.statement: Requirements live in version control (git, structured Notion, Aha!) with full history: which model version drafted, what transcript citation, which BA approved, what was edited. Throwaway AI drafts in Slack DMs / personal notes are forbidden.

rationale.source: audit-trail requirements; SOX / ISO 27001 documentation standards

rule.rationale: Audit asks "show me where this requirement came from." Version control answers in 30 seconds; Slack DMs answer never. The model version capture lets you re-run when the LLM updates and detect drift.

example.title: AI-drafted story with full citation and AC
example.code:
Story REQ-042 As a HIPAA-covered clinic admin, I want to redact patient PHI in exported audit reports, So that I can fulfill internal compliance review without leaking PHI.
Acceptance Criteria: AC-042-1 Given an audit report is generated with patient PHI present, When the admin downloads the report, Then PHI fields (name, MRN, DOB, address) are masked as *** And a redaction-event audit log entry is created.
AC-042-2 Given the admin has the "compliance-officer" role, When they download the same report, Then PHI is shown unredacted And a privileged-access audit log entry is created.
Evidence: workshop-2026-03-14.md L 78-92 (clinic admin verbatim: "I'd rather see asterisks than risk leaking a name to a vendor consultant") process-doc-current.md L 14 (current export has no redaction at all — gap)
Compliance cross-check: HIPAA Safeguards rule §164.312(a) — supports requirement (encryption at rest AND access controls); does not conflict. Verified by BA against constraint list.
Review: BA: J. Park (signed 2026-03-17 09:22) Model: claude-opus-4-7 drafted 2026-03-16 23:11 Edits made: AC-042-2 added by BA (LLM missed privileged-access path)
example.note: Illustrates r1 (citations), r2 (BABOK format), r3 (compliance check), r4 (review log), r5 (model version).

example.title: Uncited AI prose requirement
example.code:
"The system should be secure and compliant with applicable regulations."
Problem: violates r1 (no citation), r2 (not BABOK format), r3 (no compliance cross-check), unactionable. LLM generated it from general "secure software" training, not from elicitation. Repair: reject. Re-run AI draft pointing it at specific transcripts. Or remove from spec entirely; security NFRs need explicit threat model.
example.note: "Secure and compliant" is the canonical filler requirement that always means nothing.
